---
title: "LLM evaluation, honestly"
description: "Why your eval scores look good while the system stays unreliable, and what to measure instead."
image: 05-evaluation.png
image-alt: "Cover image for the series: LLM evaluation, honestly"
order: 5
parts: 7
date: 2026-05-15
toc: false
listing:
  contents:
    - "*/index.qmd"
  sort: "order"
  type: default
  categories: false
  sort-ui: false
  filter-ui: false
  fields: [title, date, description]
  feed:
    title: "LLM evaluation, honestly — series"
    description: "Why your eval scores look good while the system stays unreliable, and what to measure instead."
---

The series treats LLM evaluation as engineering: labels built from real traces, code checks before LLM judges, judges treated as calibrated classifiers, scores read as samples not truth, and an eval system you maintain like any other. The point is to know whether the system is actually getting better.

Read in order:
